title: soil-rs-correlation-screening: code and reproducible outputs for the leakage-controlled screening of 512 remote-sensing features against agrochemical soil properties (Northern Kazakhstan steppe)

description:
Code and reproducible outputs for the correlation-screening study Digital Soil Mapping of the Steppe Zone of Northern Kazakhstan: Relationships Between Agrochemical Soil Properties and Multimodal Remote Sensing Data. This is the feature-screening / feature-quality companion of the machine- and deep-learning modelling paper (doi:10.3390/agriculture16111239), with which it shares only the underlying soil dataset and no results or manuscript text.

Study. 1,085 topsoil samples (0-25 cm) from 20 farms and 81 fields across the Northern Kazakhstan steppe (2022-2023). Six agrochemical properties (pH(KCl), SOC, NO3, P2O5, K2O, S) are screened against 512 multimodal remote-sensing features (Sentinel-2, Landsat-8, Sentinel-1 SAR, SRTM and ERA5-Land via Google Earth Engine; 140 spectral indices over four seasonal windows) plus 110 composite features.

Method. Spearman screening with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR under leakage control (temporal alignment and out-of-farm Farm-LOFO cross-validation, 20 folds), plus nested variance components, spatial-autocorrelation analysis (Moran's I with declustering), pH-confounding and an empirical feature taxonomy (generalizable / zonal-only / unstable / weak).

Key result. A property's honest out-of-farm predictability scales with its between-field variance share (ICC): Spearman rho(ICC, out-of-farm) = +0.83. The high-structure properties (pH, K2O, P2O5, SOC) generalise, whereas sulfur - the property with the lowest between-field structure (ICC = 0.17) - does not (out-of-farm rho ~ 0.04), so its high in-sample correlation is a signature of leakage rather than signal.

Contents. Analysis code (math_statistics/, ML/) and the reproducible result tables and figures; deterministic (SEED = 42) and byte-reproducible.

Data note. The raw sample-level soil laboratory data are NOT included here (commercially sensitive; the same data as the companion study, archived separately - see Related identifiers). The analysis is fully reproducible from that dataset as described in the README.
notes: Raw sample-level soil laboratory data (master_dataset_old.csv, soil_analysis.xlsx) are deliberately excluded from this archive because they are commercially sensitive; the analysis is fully reproducible from that private dataset per the README.
